title: Grammar Rules
editorialSummary: Grammatical categories grouped by type. The existing pool has 'Parts of Speech' as a label — this puzzle avoids that label entirely. False trail: Nominative and Accusative look like they could be Parts of Speech to players unfamiliar with Latin grammar. Gerund is a Verb Form that functions like a noun, tempting players to place it in a Parts-of-Speech group. Supine is a Latin verbal noun (a verb form) that is largely obsolete in modern English — included to add challenge without being obscure in a grammar context.

meta.gptRationale: Grammar terminology is abstract and several terms are easy to confuse unless the player has formal linguistics background.
